title: selinum ranunculoides
intro: Selinum ranunculoides, also known as Wild Angelica or Lesser Celery-leaved Angelica, is a biennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Selinum ranunculoides include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Selinum ranunculoides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
